county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Freestone County formed 1850 from Limestone. County seat is Fairfield. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Fairfield. Central Texas county. Freestone County Historical Commission. Cotton economy. Freedmen's Bureau records. Check Limestone for pre-1850 records. Fairfield Lake State Park area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Freestone County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1850; County Clerk in Fairfield holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Freestone County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1850 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Freestone County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1850. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Freestone County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1850. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Freestone County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Freestone County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Freestone County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Freestone County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Freestone County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Freestone County from 1850. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Frio County formed 1858 from Atascosa, Bexar, Uvalde. County seat is Pearsall. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Pearsall. South Texas brush country. Frio County Historical Society. Winter Garden area farming. Mexican-American heritage. Check Atascosa, Bexar, Uvalde for pre-1858 records.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1900 hurricane caused significant damage to some records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Galveston County formed 1838 from Brazoria. County seat is Galveston. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1900 hurricane caused significant damage to some records.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Galveston. Gulf Coast island county — major 19th-century port. 1900 hurricane (deadliest natural disaster in US history) caused some record damage. Galveston and Texas History Center at Rosenberg Library is the major repository. Juneteenth originated here (June 19, 1865). Immigration through Galveston port (Galveston Movement, 1907-1914 — Jewish immigrants). Galveston Historical Foundation. Significant African American community records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Galveston County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1838; County Clerk in Galveston holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Garza County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Post. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Post. South Plains county organized 1907. C.W. Post (cereal magnate) founded Post City as a utopian colony (1907). Post settled by company town model — detailed company records. Garza County Historical Museum. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Garza County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Post holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Gillespie County formed 1848 from Bexar, Travis. County seat is Fredericksburg. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Fredericksburg. Hill Country county — the center of German immigration in Texas. Vereins Kirche Museum. Pioneer Museum Complex. German-language church records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist). Gillespie County Historical Society. Admiral Nimitz Museum (WWII). Meusebach-Comanche Treaty (1847) records at TSLAC. Wine industry records. Exceptional documentation of German immigrant communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Gillespie County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1848; County Clerk in Fredericksburg holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Gillespie County Deed Records -- County Clerk

source_profiles.source_name: Gillespie County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Gillespie County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Gillespie County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the Gillespie County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Gillespie County German Church Records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist)
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / TSLAC / Texas Lutheran University archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German immigrant church records including baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Gillespie County has significant German settlement heritage. German-language records may require translation. Check local Lutheran, Catholic, and Methodist congregations.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Gillespie County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Gillespie County from 1848. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Goliad County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Goliad). County seat is Goliad. Gulf Coast region with port, plantation, and petrochemical heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Goliad. Original Texas county — La Bahia Presidio and Mission Espiritu Santo. Spanish colonial records (Franciscan mission registers). Goliad State Park. Site of Goliad Massacre (1836). Check Catholic Church sacramental records from 1749+. Goliad County Historical Commission. One of the most historically significant Texas counties.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Goliad County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Goliad holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Goliad County Deed Records -- County Clerk

source_profiles.source_name: Goliad County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Goliad County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Goliad County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Goliad County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Goliad County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Goliad County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Goliad County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Gonzales County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Gonzales). County seat is Gonzales. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Gonzales. Original Texas county — site of the first battle of the Texas Revolution ('Come and Take It,' 1835). Gonzales Memorial Museum. DeWitt Colony empresario records at GLO. Gonzales Pioneer Village. Cotton and cattle heritage. Check for Mexican-era records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Gonzales County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Gonzales holds deed books
